methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Avalanche Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Robin Langtry that has raised $80M to date. The valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with three same-sector, same-stage private companies—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—forming the primary peer set.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $80M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, implying a $160M valuation. The peer-median method references the three-company cohort, which yields a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M to $200M. Both methods are applicable given the target's stage and sector positioning.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a low estimate of $128M, a median of $155M, and a high of $184M. The narrow convergence between stage-progression ($160M) and peer-median ($150M) suggests moderate internal consistency. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three-peer sample, performance and product maturity relative to each comparable, and capital efficiency relative to raised capital. The analysis supports a valuation range of $150M–$160M, with $155M as the midpoint.
